name: Cranberry-Maple Sauce
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 cups Cranberries (if frozen, do not thaw)
recipeInstructions:
1. BRING THE MAPLE SYRUP and sugar to a boil in a small non-aluminum pan.
2. Cook for 3 minutes until boiling.
3. Add the cranberries and cook until their skins burst and they begin to pop, about 6-to-8 minutes. If frozen, cook slightly longer.
4. Cut the butter into three pieces.
5. Remove the pan from the heat and whisk in the butter one piece at a time, waiting for each to be fully incorporated before adding another.
6. Add the bourbon.
7. The sauce can be served immediately or refrigerated for up to a week. To serve, gently reheat and thin with 2 to 3 tablespoons water. Makes 1 1/4 Cups
